Batistuta's cannon for Fiorentina? 152 Serie A goals, many from pure thunderbolts, carrying a team. Van Nistelrooy at United? 95 PL goals in 150 games, absolute penalty box royalty. One thrived on spectacle, the other on cold, hard efficiency. Which pure striker legend gives you the shivers still?

"Think 'parking the bus' is a modern invention?"

Helenio Herrera's Inter Milan in the 60s mastered Catenaccio! It wasn't just defense; it was a lethal system of a libero, man-marking, and devastating counter-attacks. Two European Cups, three Scudetti. They didn't just win; they dictated football's defensive future. Genius.
